af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Okta is an identity platform (OAuth 2.0/OIDC provider) with excellent developer docs at developer.okta.com. auth_complexity_score=40 because Okta IS the auth provider — integrating it requires implementing OAuth 2.0/OIDC flows, which are inherently complex. Error codes are well-documented. Org-based rate limits clearly explained.

security_score_components.security_notes: Okta is an enterprise-grade identity platform with mandatory HTTPS, OAuth 2.0/OIDC, PKCE, MFA enforcement, and fine-grained OAuth scopes. Industry-leading security posture as a security product. Secrets managed via API tokens with rotation support.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: status.okta.com with SLA commitments. Stable versioned API. Deprecation notices issued well in advance. Retry guidance and idempotency documented in developer guides.

af_score_components.notes: Has official MCP server for 1Password Secrets Automation. Good developer docs at developer.1password.com. Service account tokens for programmatic access (OAuth-level complexity). Rate limits documented but not heavily emphasized.

security_score_components.security_notes: Purpose-built secrets management platform — security is the core product. Mandatory HTTPS, service account tokens with scoped vault access, biometric + MFA for human access. Best-in-class secret handling by design. Security-focused team with strong track record.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page available. Stable versioned API with backwards compatibility. Secrets Automation API is mature. Retry guidance documented. As a security product, uptime is critical to their business.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. OneSignal push notification and multi-channel messaging platform. Good REST API documentation at documentation.onesignal.com. Simple API key + App ID authentication. Error codes documented. Rate limits covered but not prominently featured.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key + App ID authentication — functional but limited granularity. No fine-grained scope control per endpoint. Maintained platform with reasonable security posture. API keys should be kept in env vars.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page available. Stable REST API with versioning. Breaking change history is reasonable. Basic retry guidance for delivery failures. Used at scale by many mobile apps.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Onfido KYC/identity verification API with good documentation at documentation.onfido.com. Token-based authentication (JWT, API tokens). Sandbox and live environments separate. Error codes well-documented. Rate limits documented per plan.

security_score_components.security_notes: KYC compliance requires strong security — Onfido delivers. Mandatory HTTPS, JWT authentication, sandbox/live separation, GDPR compliance built-in. Webhook signatures for verification callbacks. Security-first approach appropriate for identity verification use case.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SLA available for enterprise. Versioned API with v2/v3 migration guides. Retry handling documented for async verification workflows. Webhook-based result delivery adds resilience.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server (community MCP wrappers exist). Free, open-source weather API with excellent interactive documentation at open-meteo.com/en/docs. No authentication required for free tier — auth_complexity_score=100 (simplest possible). Rate limits clearly documented per tier. Error responses basic but sufficient.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS supported and recommended but open API by design. No authentication for free tier means no access control — appropriate for public weather data. Optional API key for commercial use. Open source codebase allows dependency audit. No sensitive data handled.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: No formal SLA for free tier. Stable API with good backwards compatibility history. Open-source community project with consistent maintenance. Basic retry guidance. Multiple data source backends add resilience.

af_score_components.notes: No official MCP server. Excellent documentation at platform.openai.com/docs with SDKs in major languages. Simple API key authentication. Comprehensive error codes (rate limits, context length, auth errors, etc.). Tiered rate limits documented by model and plan. Note: model deprecations require migration planning.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. API key authentication with project-scoped keys, org-level management, and key rotation UI. Some scope control via project keys. Maintained by security-conscious team. Docs recommend env var storage for API keys. SOC 2 Type II certified.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: status.openai.com with incident history. Stable API v1 but model deprecations create migration burden — migration guides provided but timelines can be short. Retry-After headers documented. Exponential backoff guidance in SDKs. High traffic periods can cause latency spikes.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. UK Open Banking standard (OBIE/FAPI spec). Good standards documentation at openbanking.org.uk but implementation varies by bank. auth_complexity_score=30 (very complex) — requires OAuth 2.0 FAPI, eIDAS qualified certificates, dynamic client registration, and per-bank integration. Rate limits vary by bank — not standardized.

security_score_components.security_notes: Highest security standards by regulation. Mutual TLS mandatory. OAuth 2.0 FAPI with mandatory PKCE and JWS signatures. Fine-grained account/payment/transaction permissions with explicit PSU consent. eIDAS certificate-based client authentication — no shared secrets. PSD2/FCA regulated.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: No single SLA — varies by bank TPP agreements. v3.x standard is stable. Standards body manages versioning with migration guidance. Retry guidance in spec. Resilience varies by bank implementation. Sandbox environments available for testing.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Forward/reverse geocoding API with excellent documentation at opencagedata.com/api. Simple API key authentication. Comprehensive error codes (400, 401, 402, 403, 429) clearly documented. Tier-based rate limits with free tier clearly explained. Good SDK support in multiple languages.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key authentication — functional but single-level access control. No fine-grained scope control. Publicly available geocoding data with no sensitive PII processed. Recommends API key in server-side requests only (not client-side).

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page available. Stable v1 API with strong backwards compatibility commitment. Multiple data source backends (OSM, etc.). Clear retry guidance for rate limit errors (429). SLA available on paid plans.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Open-source data catalog platform (sandbox.open-metadata.org for API explorer). Comprehensive but complex API surface area. auth_complexity_score=45 — supports JWT, SAML, LDAP, OIDC; complex enterprise auth setup. Rate limits not well-documented (self-hosted). API docs functional but dense.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS recommended but self-hosted deployments vary. Supports SAML, LDAP, OIDC, JWT — strong auth options available. Team and role-based access control. Open source means dependency audit needed by operator. Connection credentials (databases, warehouses) stored in platform — handling quality depends on deployment.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — no SLA (cloud managed version available). Semantic versioning with migration scripts between versions. Some breaking changes between major versions with migration guides. Basic retry patterns. Reliability depends on operator's infrastructure choices.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. FinOps automation platform — relatively newer entrant. Documentation less mature than established APIs. API key authentication with some workflow complexity. Rate limits not prominently documented. Error handling basic. Platform targets FinOps/cloud cost optimization workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced for cloud service. API key authentication — functional but limited. Some resource-level permission control. Newer platform means security posture still maturing. Handles cloud provider credentials for cost analysis — secret handling practices need operator verification.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Newer platform with limited reliability track record. No formal published SLA. Early-stage versioning. Limited breaking change history to assess. Basic error recovery patterns. Platform is maturing — reliability should improve over time.
